description: §12.4 — the SPLIT form (the resolution E-ROUTE-005 suggests) is clean: a client fn does the DOM access and calls a separate server fn for the ?{} work (client -> server, §12.3). No single function accesses both sides, so E-ROUTE-005 does not fire; and the only cross-boundary call is client -> server, so E-ROUTE-002 does not fire either. Negative control guarding the false-positive direction.
rationale: §12.4 constrains only unplaceable single functions and server -> client-only calls. Splitting the concerns (server-only work in a server fn, DOM in a client fn that calls it) is a valid, placeable program — neither E-ROUTE-005 nor E-ROUTE-002 fires.
